title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
summary: Google Cloud presented Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration methods for standardizing how agents connect to data sources like BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB. The session covered building agents with a single workflow and implementing AgentOps for observability and cost monitoring with minimal code.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech session demonstrates how the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) standardize the way agents connect to data sources including BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB. It matters because a common protocol reduces the bespoke wiring usually needed to link agents to multiple backends. Concrete takeaways: agents can be built within a single workflow, and AgentOps for observability and cost monitoring can be implemented with minimal code.
